title: Turkle conversation erosion loop
summary: Algorithmically-mediated communication trains fragmented attention and lowers tolerance for silence, eroding face-to-face conversation skill. Reduced skill makes in-person interaction less rewarding, driving still more mediated communication — a reinforcing loop Turkle calls the conversation crisis.
source: Ch09 §9.3 — Turkle interview; "technology coordinates with our habits in ways that make us worse at human connection."

edges.description: Weaker skill makes face-to-face less rewarding → more mediated use

identifiability_note: Closed observational loop; no latent confounder modeled. Intervention on mediated-communication exposure would be needed to estimate magnitudes.
